name: Ashcroft v. Al-Kidd
question: Is the former government official entitled to qualified immunity from the pretext claim based on the conclusions that (a) the Fourth Amendment prohibits an officer from executing a valid material witness warrant with the intent of conducting further investigation or preventively detaining the subject; and (b) this Fourth Amendment rule was clearly established at the time of Al-Kidd's arrest?
description: A case in which the Court held that former Attorney General John Ashcroft was entitled to qualified immunity.
